rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION, A NON-PROFIT VS. CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, May 19, 2025, Line 1. 2 - RESPONDENTs CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD, A PUBLIC AGENCY,and ERIC OPEENHEIMER Notice Of Hearing And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief. Continued to June 12, 2025 on the court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANN MARIE URBANSKI VS. EPSILON DATA MANAGEMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Approving Settlement Pursuant To California Labor Code Private Attorneys General Act And Entering Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof - Redacted
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, May 19, 2025, Line 12. 1 - PLAINTIFF ANN URBANSKI's Motion For Order Approving Settlement Pursuant To California Labor Code Private Attorneys General Act And Entering Judgment. - Redacted Plaintiff Ann Marie Urbanski's Motion For Order Approving Settlement Pursuant To California Labor Code Private Attorneys General Act And Entering Judgment, which Defendant joins, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Good cause appearing and notice having been provided to LWDA, the court approves the parties' settlement of the PAGA claims. (See Lab. Code, 2699(s).) The court denies the request to enter judgment because no clear argument was made on that point and no proposed judgment was submitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE VS. EUR-ASIA AUTO BODY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gd Towing Auto Service Service Llc'S Written Discovery Desponses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, May 19, 2025, Line 8. 2 - PLAINTIFF CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Compel Defendant Gd Towing Auto Service Service Llc'S Written Discovery Desponses And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange's Motion To Compel To Compel Defendant GD Towing Auto Service LLC's Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. Good cause appearing and pursuant to the California Discovery Act, no later than 15 days after notice of this order, Defendant GD Towing Auto Service LLC must provide verified, objection free responses to the following discovery served by Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange on November 26, 2024: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set One and (2) Requests for Production, Set One to GD Towing. The record does not show Defendant GD Towing Auto Service LLC acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant GD Towing Auto Service LLC shall pay $1,650 to Plaintiff as sanctions, payment no later than June 13, 2025. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE VS. EUR-ASIA AUTO BODY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body Llc'S Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, May 19, 2025, Line 8. 1 - PLAINTIFF CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Compel Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body Llc'S Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange's Motion To Compel Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body LLC's Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. Good cause appearing and pursuant to the California Discovery Act, no later than 15 days after notice of this order, Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body LLC must provide verified, objection free responses to the following discovery served by Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange on November 26, 2024: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set One, (2) Special Interrogatories, Set One, and (3) Requests for Production, Set One to Eur-Asia. The record does not show Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body LLC acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Eur-Asia Auto Body LLC shall pay $1,650 to Plaintiff as sanctions, payment no later than June 13, 2025. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMUNDO U. HERNADEZ ET AL VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, May 19, 2025, Line 5. DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings. Defendant Nissan North America, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Arbitration and to Stay Proceedings is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
